action.reasoning: Observation: 4000-row LoRA training improved val from 24% (200-row) to 25% on first 150. Marginal improvement - going from 200→4000 rows didn't help much. mean_iou is 0.22. Analysis: The model is converging but on training distribution (ShowUI=full UI elements with large bboxes), while val is also ShowUI distribution. Test is ScreenSpot-Pro. With 8.7h left, 18 iters, I should: (1) submit current ckpt as baseline to see real test score (1 iter, ~12min), then (2) train more carefully with maybe 2 epochs. Actually the test set might use point evaluation - target_point. Let me first submit eval to validate pipeline and see actual score. Decision: Submit eval now to get real test score baseline, then iterate.
